offers.description: Flat-rate AC repair pricing in Fort Lauderdale: diagnostic $79-$129, capacitor $150-$300, refrigerant recharge $250-$650, compressor $1,200-$2,400.

mainEntity.name: How much does AC repair cost in Fort Lauderdale?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most AC repairs in Fort Lauderdale cost $150 to $650, averaging around $300 for common fixes like a failed capacitor, contactor, or low refrigerant. A flat $79-$129 diagnostic fee applies and is credited toward the repair when you book with House AC Repair.

mainEntity.name: How much does AC repair cost in Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: Across Florida, AC repair averages $200 to $700, slightly higher than the national average because year-round cooling means harder-working systems and more refrigerant and compressor calls. Fort Lauderdale pricing sits in the middle of that range with flat-rate quotes.

mainEntity.name: How much does an AC refrigerant recharge cost in Fort Lauderdale?

acceptedAnswer.text: An AC refrigerant recharge in Fort Lauderdale costs $250 to $650 depending on refrigerant type. R-410A systems are cheaper than older R-22 units. A low charge means a leak, so a proper repair seals the leak first before refilling.

mainEntity.name: What is the average cost to fix an AC not blowing cold air?

acceptedAnswer.text: The average cost to fix an AC not blowing cold air in Fort Lauderdale is $150 to $650. Common causes are a bad capacitor ($150-$300), low refrigerant from a leak ($250-$650), a frozen evaporator coil ($200-$500), or a failed contactor ($150-$350).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth repairing an AC unit or should you replace it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Repairing is usually worth it when your AC is under 10 years old and the fix costs less than $500. Use the $5,000 rule: multiply the repair cost by the unit's age; if it exceeds $5,000, lean toward replacement.

mainEntity.name: What does an AC repair diagnostic fee include?

acceptedAnswer.text: A House AC Repair diagnostic fee of $79-$129 covers a full inspection: refrigerant pressure check, electrical and capacitor testing, airflow and coil assessment, and a written flat-rate quote. The fee is waived or credited toward the repair when you approve the work the same visit.
